heading: Certain collective activities; exemption from antitrust laws
label: 13703 Certain collective activities; exemption from antitrust laws

Amendments2003—Subsecs. (d) to (h). redesignated subsecs. (e) to (h) as (d) to (g), respectively, and struck out heading and text of former subsec. (d). Text read as follows: “The Board shall not take any action that would permit the establishment of nationwide collective ratemaking authority.”Pub. L. 108–71999—Subsec. (c). , designated introductory provisions as par. (1) and inserted heading, redesignated former pars. (1) to (4) as subpars. (A) to (D), respectively, of par. (1) and realigned their margins, and added par. (2).Pub. L. 106–159, § 227(a)Subsec. (d). , amended heading and text of subsec. (d) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Subject to subsection (c), approval of an agreement under subsection (a) shall expire 3 years after the date of approval unless renewed under this subsection. The approval may be renewed upon request of the parties to the agreement if such parties resubmit the agreement to the Board, the agreement is unchanged, and the Board approves such renewal. The Board shall approve the renewal unless it finds that the renewal is not in the public interest. Parties to the agreement may continue to undertake activities pursuant to the previously approved agreement while the renewal request is pending.”Pub. L. 106–159, § 227(b)Subsec. children.label: A
children.text: through routes and joint rates;

children.label: B
children.text: rates for the transportation of household goods;

children.label: C
children.text: classifications;

children.label: D
children.text: mileage guides;

children.label: E
children.text: rules;

children.label: F
children.text: divisions;

children.label: G
children.text: rate adjustments of general application based on industry average carrier costs (so long as there is no discussion of individual markets or particular single-line rates); or

children.label: H
children.text: procedures for joint consideration, initiation, or establishment of matters described in subparagraphs (A) through (G).

children.heading: .—Submission of agreement to board; approval
children.label: 2 .—Submission of agreement to board; approval
children.text: An agreement entered into under paragraph (1) may be submitted by any carrier or carriers that are parties to such agreement to the Board for approval and may be approved by the Board only if it finds that such agreement is in the public interest.

children.heading: .—Conditions
children.label: 3 .—Conditions
children.text: The Board may require compliance with reasonable conditions consistent with this part to assure that the agreement furthers the transportation policy set forth in section 13101.

children.heading: .—Independently established rates
children.label: 4 .—Independently established rates
children.text: Any carrier which is a party to an agreement under paragraph (1) is not, and may not be, precluded from independently establishing its own rates, classification, and mileages or from adopting and using a noncollectively made classification or mileage guide.

children.heading: .—Reasonableness
children.label: A .—Reasonableness
children.text: The Board may suspend and investigate the reasonableness of any rate, rule, classification, or rate adjustment of general application made pursuant to an agreement under this section.

children.heading: .—Actions not in the public interest
children.label: B .—Actions not in the public interest
children.text: The Board may investigate any action taken pursuant to an agreement approved under this section. If the Board finds that the action is not in the public interest, the Board may take such measures as may be necessary to protect the public interest with regard to the action, including issuing an order directing the parties to cease and desist or modify the action.

children.heading: .—Effect of approval
children.label: 6 .—Effect of approval
children.text: If the Board approves the agreement or renews approval of the agreement, it may be made and carried out under its terms and under the conditions required by the Board, and the antitrust laws, as defined in the first section of the Clayton Act (), do not apply to parties and other persons with respect to making or carrying out the agreement.15 U.S.C. 12

children.heading: .—Records
children.label: b .—Records
children.text: The Board may require an organization established or continued under an agreement approved under this section to maintain records and submit reports. The Board, or its delegate, may inspect a record maintained under this section, or monitor any organization’s compliance with this section.

children.label: A
children.text: approving an agreement,

children.label: B
children.text: denying, ending, or changing approval,

children.label: C
children.text: prescribing the conditions on which approval is granted, or

children.label: D
children.text: changing those conditions,

children.heading: .—Periodic review of approvals
children.label: 2 .—Periodic review of approvals
children.text: Subject to this section, in the 5-year period beginning on the date of the enactment of this paragraph and in each 5-year period thereafter, the Board shall initiate a proceeding to review any agreement approved pursuant to this section. Any such agreement shall be continued unless the Board determines otherwise.

children.heading: .—Agreements existing as ofDecember 31, 1995
children.label: 1 .—Agreements existing as ofDecember 31, 1995
children.text: Agreements approved under former section 10706(b) and in effect on , shall be treated for purposes of this section as approved by the Board under this section beginning on .December 31, 1995January 1, 1996

children.heading: .—Cases pending as of date of the enactment
children.label: 2 .—Cases pending as of date of the enactment
children.text: Nothing in section 227 (other than subsection (b)) of the Motor Carrier Safety Improvement Act of 1999, including the amendments made by such section, shall be construed to affect any case brought under this section that is pending before the Board as of the date of the enactment of this paragraph.

children.heading: .—Undercharge claims
children.label: 1 .—Undercharge claims
children.text: Nothing in this section shall serve as a basis for any undercharge claim.

children.heading: .—Obligation of shipper
children.label: 2 .—Obligation of shipper
children.text: Nothing in this title, the ICC Termination Act of 1995, or any amendments or repeals made by such Act shall be construed as creating any obligation for a shipper based solely on a classification that was on file with the Interstate Commerce Commission or elsewhere on .December 31, 1995

children.heading: .—Public availability
children.label: A .—Public availability
children.text: Routes, rates, classifications, mileage guides, and rules established under agreements approved under this section shall be published and made available for public inspection upon request.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: A motor carrier of property whose routes, rates, classifications, mileage guides, rules, or packaging are determined or governed by publications established under agreements approved under this section must participate in the determining or governing publication for such provisions to apply.

children.heading: .—Power of attorney
children.label: ii .—Power of attorney
children.text: The motor carrier of property shall issue a power of attorney to the publishing agent and, upon its acceptance, the agent shall issue a written certification to the motor carrier affirming its participation in the governing publication, and the certification shall be made available for public inspection.

children.label: A
children.text: is a participant in a publication of mileages formulated under an agreement approved under this section; or

children.label: B
children.text: uses a publication of mileage (other than a publication described in subparagraph (A)) that can be examined by any interested person upon reasonable request.

children.heading: .—Single Line Rate Defined
children.label: g .—Single Line Rate Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “single line rate” means a rate, charge, or allowance proposed by a single motor carrier that is applicable only over its line and for which the transportation can be provided by that carrier.
